markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define human, local model, cloud model, validator, and system actor/site IDs used in CRDT state and EventLedger events. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: ActorSiteIdModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ActorSiteIdModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 8463e674; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: Typed actor id '<kind>:<ident>' with a closed kind enum (operator|local_model|cloud_model|validator|system) matching the MT-041 seed vocabulary, plus strict ident validation (non-empty, <=128 chars, charset [A-Za-z0-9._-]) (actor_site.rs:139-228). serde via try_from String so PostgreSQL TEXT columns, EventLedger payloads, and JSON envelopes all round-trip the identical canonical form. This is the identity backbone that makes the spec's actor-receipt MUSTs attributable everywhere.

Site id is a DETERMINISTIC SHA256 derivation over (derivation_tag | workspace_id | crdt_document_id | actor.canonical) (derive_knowledge_site_id, actor_site.rs:246-267). Same inputs -> same site, so a restarted no-context session re-derives its site with zero local state -- exactly what swarm recovery needs. yjs_client_id = first 4 bytes of the digest so the browser Y.Doc adopts the same client id without a registration round-trip. site_id is 64-bit (16 hex) -- adequate collision resistance for per-(workspace,doc) attribution.

to_kernel_actor maps cleanly onto the existing KernelActor taxonomy (Operator/ModelAdapter/ValidationRunner/System), so CRDT-side events carry the same actor model as the rest of the kernel -- no parallel attribution vocabulary.
No sidecar / file-based actor registry: actor ids live only in PostgreSQL rows. Consistent with the no-external-authority posture.
evasion_probes: Tried an unknown actor kind ('admin:x'): UnknownActorKind rejected at parse.
Tried an empty or oversized ident, and idents with forbidden chars (space, '/', ':'): EmptyIdent / IdentTooLong / IdentBadChar rejected.

Tried to forge a foreign site id: every consumer (yjs_bridge validate, save semantics) re-derives the site from (workspace, doc, actor) and compares (SiteIdMismatch), so a spoofed site_id is caught downstream.
Tried two different idents colliding to one site: derivation includes actor.canonical(), so distinct actors get distinct digests barring SHA256 collision.
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/crdt/actor_site.rs:28-318 (actor kind, actor id, site derivation, identity builder)
Cross-checked yjs_bridge.rs:211-219 (SiteIdMismatch enforcement) and 0151/0150/0152/0153/0154/0155 actor_id regex CHECKs which pin the same '<kind>:<ident>' grammar at the DB layer

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
